006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *

010 *

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.hocon;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023import java.util.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
031
032/**
033 * Session for parsing HOCON format into POJOs.
034 */
035@SuppressWarnings({
036   "java:S110", "java:S115", "java:S135", "java:S3776", "java:S6541", "unchecked",
037   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's parser session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
038   // java:S135: parseValueOrConcat uses two distinct breaks — one for standard HOCON terminators, one for unquoted keys after arrays/objects; merging them would obscure intent
039})
040public class HoconParserSession extends ReaderParserSession implements RecordReadable {
041
042   private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
043   private static final String[] EMPTY_PATH = new String[0];
044
045   /**
046    * Builder for HOCON parser session.
047    */
048   public static class Builder extends ReaderParserSession.Builder<Builder> {
049
050      private HoconParser hoconParser;
051
052      protected Builder(HoconParser ctx) {
053         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
054         this.hoconParser = ctx;
055      }
056
057      @Override
058      public HoconParserSession build() {
059         return new HoconParserSession(this);
060      }
061   }
062
063   /**
064    * Creates a session builder.
065    *
066    * @param ctx The parser context.
067    * @return The builder.
068    */
069   public static Builder create(HoconParser ctx) {
070      return new Builder(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
071   }
072
073   private final HoconParser hoconParser;
074
075   protected HoconParserSession(Builder builder) {
076      super(builder);
077      hoconParser = builder.hoconParser;
078   }
079
080   @Override /* RecordReadable */
081   public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
082      return RecordAdapter.reader(this, input);
083   }
084
085   @Override /* RecordReadable */
086   public boolean isRecordStreaming() {
087      return false;
088   }
089
090   @Override
091   protected <T> T doParse(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
092      String inputStr;
093      try {
094         inputStr = pipe.asString();
095      } catch (IOException e) {
096         throw new ParseException(e);
097      }
098      if (inputStr == null)
099         return null;
100      var trimmed = inputStr.trim();
101      if (trimmed.isEmpty())
102         return null;
103      var tokenizer = new HoconTokenizer(new StringReader(inputStr));
104      var root = parseRoot(tokenizer);
105      if (root == null)
106         return null;
107      if (hoconParser.resolveSubstitutions) {
108         var resolver = new HoconResolver(root);
109         resolver.resolve();
110      }
111      var map = hoconToMap(root, type);
112      return (T) convertToBean(map, type);
113   }
114
115   private HoconValue.HoconObject parseRoot(HoconTokenizer t) throws IOException, ParseException {
116      t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
117      var peek = t.peek();
118      if (peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.EOF)
119         return null;
120      if (peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.LBRACE) {
121         t.read();
122         return parseObject(t, null, new String[0]);
123      }
124      return parseRootBraceless(t);
125   }
126
127   private HoconValue.HoconObject parseRootBraceless(HoconTokenizer t) throws IOException, ParseException {
128      var root = new HoconValue.HoconObject();
129      while (t.peek().type() != HoconTokenizer.TokenType.EOF) {
130         var path = readPath(t);
131         if (path == null || path.length == 0)
132            break;
133         t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
134         var next = t.peek();
135         if (next.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.LBRACE) {
136            t.read();
137            var nested = parseObject(t, root, path);
138            var existing = root.getPath(path);
139            if (existing instanceof HoconValue.HoconObject existingObj)
140               existingObj.merge(nested);
141            else
142               root.setPath(path, nested);
143         } else if (next.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.PLUS_EQUALS) {
144            t.read();
145            t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
146            var value = parseValueOrConcat(t);
147            var existing = root.getPath(path);
148            if (existing instanceof HoconValue.HoconArray arr)
149               arr.getElements().add(value);
150            else {
151               var newArr = new HoconValue.HoconArray();
152               if (existing != null)
153                  newArr.getElements().add(existing);
154               newArr.getElements().add(value);
155               root.setPath(path, newArr);
156            }
157         } else if (next.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.EQUALS || next.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.COLON) {
158            t.read();
159            t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
160            var value = parseValueOrConcat(t);
161            value = resolveSelfRefConcatIfNeeded(value, path, root);
162            root.setPath(path, value);
163         } else {
164            throw new ParseException(this, "Expected =, : or brace at line {0}", t.getLine());
165         }
166         t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
167         if (t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.NEWLINE)
168            t.read();
169      }
170      return root;
171   }
172
173   private String[] readPath(HoconTokenizer t) throws IOException, ParseException {
174      var tok = t.read();
175      String first = switch (tok.type()) {
176         case EOF -> null;
177         case UNQUOTED_STRING -> isTrimStrings() ? tok.stringValue().trim() : tok.stringValue();
178         case QUOTED_STRING, TRIPLE_QUOTED -> isTrimStrings() ? tok.stringValue().trim() : tok.stringValue();
179         case NULL -> null;
180         case TRUE -> "true";
181         case FALSE -> "false";
182         case NUMBER -> tok.numberValue().toString();
183         default -> throw new ParseException(this, "Expected key at line {0}", t.getLine());
184      };
185      if (first == null)
186         return EMPTY_PATH;
187      var path = new ArrayList<String>();
188      if (tok.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.UNQUOTED_STRING && first.contains(".") && !first.startsWith("."))
189         path.addAll(Arrays.asList(first.split("\\.", -1)));
190      else {
191         var comp = first.startsWith(".") ? first.substring(1) : first;
192         if (!comp.isEmpty())
193            path.add(comp);
194         t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
195         while (t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.UNQUOTED_STRING
196               || t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.QUOTED_STRING
197               || t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.TRIPLE_QUOTED) {
198            var next = t.read().stringValue();
199            var part = next.startsWith(".") ? next.substring(1) : next;
200            if (!part.isEmpty())
201               path.add(part);
202            t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
203         }
204      }
205      return path.toArray(String[]::new);
206   }
207
208   /**
209    * Parses a value, supporting HOCON concatenation: adjacent values on the same line
210    * are concatenated (strings) or merged (objects/arrays).
211    */
212   private HoconValue parseValueOrConcat(HoconTokenizer t) throws IOException, ParseException {
213      var values = new ArrayList<HoconValue>();
214      values.add(parseValue(t));
215      while (true) {
216         t.skipWhitespaceAndCommentsExceptNewlines();
217         var peek = t.peekNoSkip();
218         if (peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.NEWLINE
219               || peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.COMMA
220               || peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.RBRACE
221               || peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.RBRACKET
222               || peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.EQUALS
223               || peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.PLUS_EQUALS
224               || peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.EOF)
225            break;
226         // HOCON: newline ends concatenation; unquoted string after array/object is next key, not concat
227         var last = values.isEmpty() ? null : values.get(values.size() - 1);
228         if (peek.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.UNQUOTED_STRING
229               && (last instanceof HoconValue.HoconArray || last instanceof HoconValue.HoconObject))
230            break;
231         values.add(parseValue(t));
232      }
233      if (values.size() == 1)
234         return values.get(0);
235      return combineConcat(values);
236   }
237
238   private static HoconValue combineConcat(List<HoconValue> values) {
239      if (values.size() == 1)
240         return values.get(0);
241      var allArrays = values.stream().allMatch(v -> v instanceof HoconValue.HoconArray);
242      var allObjects = values.stream().allMatch(v -> v instanceof HoconValue.HoconObject);
243      if (allArrays) {
244         var first = (HoconValue.HoconArray) values.get(0);
245         for (var i = 1; i < values.size(); i++)
246            first.concat((HoconValue.HoconArray) values.get(i));
247         return first;
248      }
249      if (allObjects) {
250         var first = (HoconValue.HoconObject) values.get(0);
251         for (var i = 1; i < values.size(); i++)
252            first.merge((HoconValue.HoconObject) values.get(i));
253         return first;
254      }
255      var concat = new HoconValue.HoconConcat();
256      for (var v : values)
257         concat.add(v);
258      return concat;
259   }
260
261   private static String[] concatenatePaths(String[] prefix, String[] path) {
262      var result = new String[prefix.length + path.length];
263      System.arraycopy(prefix, 0, result, 0, prefix.length);
264      System.arraycopy(path, 0, result, prefix.length, path.length);
265      return result;
266   }
267
268   private static HoconValue resolveSelfRefConcatIfNeeded(HoconValue value, String[] fullPath,
269         HoconValue.HoconObject root) throws ParseException {
270      if (!(value instanceof HoconValue.HoconConcat concat))
271         return value;
272      var pathStr = String.join(".", fullPath);
273      if (!concat.referencesPath(pathStr))
274         return value;
275      var existing = root.getPath(fullPath);
276      if (existing == null)
277         return value;
278      var resolver = new HoconResolver(root);
279      java.util.function.Function<String, HoconValue> lookup = p -> p.equals(pathStr) ? existing : resolver.lookup(p);
280      return resolver.resolveConcatWithLookup(concat, lookup);
281   }
282
283   private HoconValue parseValue(HoconTokenizer t) throws IOException, ParseException {
284      var tok = t.peek();
285      return switch (tok.type()) {
286         case UNQUOTED_STRING, QUOTED_STRING, TRIPLE_QUOTED -> {
287            t.read();
288            var s = isTrimStrings() ? tok.stringValue().trim() : tok.stringValue();
289            yield new HoconValue.HoconString(s);
290         }
291         case NUMBER -> {
292            t.read();
293            yield new HoconValue.HoconNumber(tok.numberValue());
294         }
295         case TRUE -> {
296            t.read();
297            yield new HoconValue.HoconBoolean(true);
298         }
299         case FALSE -> {
300            t.read();
301            yield new HoconValue.HoconBoolean(false);
302         }
303         case NULL -> {
304            t.read();
305            yield new HoconValue.HoconNull();
306         }
307         case LBRACE -> {
308            t.read();
309            yield parseObject(t, null, new String[0]);
310         }
311         case LBRACKET -> {
312            t.read();
313            yield parseArray(t);
314         }
315         case SUBSTITUTION, OPT_SUBSTITUTION -> {
316            var st = t.read();
317            yield new HoconValue.HoconSubstitution(st.stringValue(), tok.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.OPT_SUBSTITUTION);
318         }
319         case EOF -> null;
320         default -> throw new ParseException(this, "Unexpected token {0} at line {1}", tok.type(), t.getLine());
321      };
322   }
323
324   private HoconValue.HoconObject parseObject(HoconTokenizer t, HoconValue.HoconObject root, String[] pathPrefix)
325         throws IOException, ParseException {
326      var obj = new HoconValue.HoconObject();
327      var effectiveRoot = root != null ? root : obj;
328      t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
329      while (t.peek().type() != HoconTokenizer.TokenType.RBRACE && t.peek().type() != HoconTokenizer.TokenType.EOF) {
330         var path = readPath(t);
331         if (path == null || path.length == 0)
332            break;
333         t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
334         var next = t.peek();
335         if (next.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.LBRACE) {
336            t.read();
337            var nested = parseObject(t, effectiveRoot, concatenatePaths(pathPrefix, path));
338            var existing = obj.getPath(path);
339            if (existing instanceof HoconValue.HoconObject existingObj)
340               existingObj.merge(nested);
341            else
342               obj.setPath(path, nested);
343         } else if (next.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.PLUS_EQUALS) {
344            t.read();
345            t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
346            var value = parseValueOrConcat(t);
347            var existing = obj.getPath(path);
348            if (existing instanceof HoconValue.HoconArray arr)
349               arr.getElements().add(value);
350            else {
351               var newArr = new HoconValue.HoconArray();
352               if (existing != null)
353                  newArr.getElements().add(existing);
354               newArr.getElements().add(value);
355               obj.setPath(path, newArr);
356            }
357         } else if (next.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.EQUALS || next.type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.COLON) {
358            t.read();
359            t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
360            var value = parseValueOrConcat(t);
361            var fullPath = concatenatePaths(pathPrefix, path);
362            value = resolveSelfRefConcatIfNeeded(value, fullPath, effectiveRoot);
363            obj.setPath(path, value);
364         } else {
365            throw new ParseException(this, "Expected =, : or brace at line {0}", t.getLine());
366         }
367         // skipWhitespaceAndComments is a no-op while a token is cached (see HoconTokenizer), so a
368         // closing `}` already peeked by parseValueOrConcat's concat loop is preserved here and the
369         // loop terminates naturally via the while-condition above rather than chewing through a
370         // newline that belongs to an enclosing scope.
371         t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
372         if (t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.COMMA || t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.NEWLINE)
373            t.read();
374         t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
375      }
376      if (t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.RBRACE)
377         t.read();
378      return obj;
379   }
380
381   private HoconValue.HoconArray parseArray(HoconTokenizer t) throws IOException, ParseException {
382      var arr = new HoconValue.HoconArray();
383      t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
384      while (t.peek().type() != HoconTokenizer.TokenType.RBRACKET && t.peek().type() != HoconTokenizer.TokenType.EOF) {
385         // Elements inside [ ... ] are separated by COMMA or NEWLINE (handled below), so each
386         // parseValueOrConcat call returns a single element.  HOCON array-concatenation
387         // (`[a,b] [c,d]` ≡ `[a,b,c,d]`) is performed inside parseValueOrConcat when adjacent
388         // arrays appear without a separator; the result is already a flattened HoconArray at
389         // that point.  Here we must add the element as-is so nested arrays like
390         // `[[1,2,3], [4,5,6]]` (with separators) stay nested rather than flattening.
391         arr.getElements().add(parseValueOrConcat(t));
392         // parseValueOrConcat's internal concat loop calls peekNoSkip(), which eagerly consumes the
393         // closing-bracket char from the underlying reader and stashes it as peeked=RBRACKET.
394         // skipWhitespaceAndComments is a no-op while a token is cached (see HoconTokenizer), so it
395         // will NOT read past the cached `]` and eat a following newline — a newline that is either
396         // the in-array separator for the NEXT element of an enclosing array or a meaningful boundary
397         // for an outer scope.  This prevents adjacent newline-separated inner arrays like
398         // `[[1,2]\n[3,4]]` from being silently re-merged via HOCON array-concatenation above us.
399         // The loop terminates on the cached `]` via the while-condition above.
400         t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
401         if (t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.COMMA || t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.NEWLINE)
402            t.read();
403         t.skipWhitespaceAndComments();
404      }
405      if (t.peek().type() == HoconTokenizer.TokenType.RBRACKET)
406         t.read();
407      return arr;
408   }
409
410   private Object hoconToMap(HoconValue val, ClassMeta<?> type) throws ParseException {
411      if (val == null)
412         return null;
413      // Bug #12: STRING values targeting byte[] consult the configured BinaryFormat's variant
414      // BinarySwap before falling back to the default String → byte[] UTF-8 coercion that the bean
415      // binder would otherwise apply at the collection-element / top-level dispatch site.  The
416      // bean-property path is unaffected because the per-property MPP install hides the type-level
417      // default swap behind the per-property swap on the BeanPropertyMeta.
418      //
419      // Bug #11/#12 a04 residual: at BinaryFormat.NOT_SET BinarySwap.match returns 0 and no swap
420      // fires, but HoconSerializerSession emits byte[] at NOT_SET as a base64 string (to sidestep
421      // HOCON's array-concatenation flattening for nested int-array wire forms).  Decode that
422      // here as the symmetric fallback so List<byte[]> and top-level byte[] round-trip at NOT_SET.
423      if (val.getType() == HoconValue.Type.STRING && type != null && type.inner() == byte[].class) {
424         var s = ((HoconValue.HoconString) val).getValue();
425         var swap = type.getSwap(this);
426         if (swap != null)
427            return unswap(swap, s, type);
428         return Base64.getDecoder().decode(s);
429      }
430      // Look up bean property metas so nested typed-map properties (Map<K,V> with non-String K) can have
431      // their keys coerced via the converter's Map→Map path before the bean binds them (Bug #7b).
432      // Extended in Bug #12 to also surface Collection/Array property types so the ARRAY branch can
433      // thread the parent's element type into recursion and the byte[] swap dispatch above can fire
434      // at the right depth.  Other property shapes (Optional, primitive, bean) still pass through as
435      // object() so the lazy-typed conversion path stays in place.
436      var beanMeta = (type != null && type.isBean()) ? type.getBeanMeta() : null;
437      var result = switch (val.getType()) {
438         case STRING -> ((HoconValue.HoconString) val).getValue();
439         case NUMBER -> ((HoconValue.HoconNumber) val).getValue();
440         case BOOLEAN -> ((HoconValue.HoconBoolean) val).getValue();
441         case NULL -> null;
442         case OBJECT -> {
443            var obj = (HoconValue.HoconObject) val;
444            var map = newGenericMap();
445            for (var e : obj.getMembers().entrySet()) {
446               var key = e.getKey();
447               ClassMeta<?> childType = object();
448               if (beanMeta != null && key != null && !key.equals(getBeanTypePropertyName(type))) {
449                  var pMeta = beanMeta.getPropertyMeta(key);
450                  if (pMeta != null) {
451                     var cm = (ClassMeta<?>) pMeta.getBeanInfo();
452                     if (cm != null && (cm.isMap() || cm.isCollectionOrArray()))
453                        childType = cm;
454                  }
455               }
456               map.put(key, hoconToMap(e.getValue(), childType));
457            }
458            yield map;
459         }
460         case ARRAY -> {
461            var arr = (HoconValue.HoconArray) val;
462            var list = newGenericList();
463            var elType = (type != null && type.isCollectionOrArray()) ? def(type.getElementType(), object()) : object();
464            for (var el : arr.getElements()) {
465               list.add(hoconToMap(el, elType));
466            }
467            yield list;
468         }
469         default -> null;
470      };
471      // If the enclosing target is a typed Map (e.g. Map<TestEnum,String>), coerce keys / values
472      // via the converter's findMapConversion so the bean property binder gets a properly-keyed map.
473      if (result instanceof Map<?,?> m && type != null && type.isMap()
474            && type.getKeyType() != null && !type.getKeyType().isObject())
475         return convertToMemberType(null, m, type);
476      return result;
477   }
478
479   private Object convertToBean(Object map, ClassMeta<?> type) throws ExecutableException, ParseException {
480      if (map == null)
481         return null;
482      if (type == null)
483         type = object();
484      var casted = cast((MarshalledMap) map, null, type);
485      if (casted != map)
486         return casted;
487      if (!type.isObject()) {
488         var swap = type.getSwap(this);
489         if (nn(swap)) {
490            var sType = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
491            var swapped = convertToMemberType(null, map, sType);
492            return unswap(swap, swapped, type);
493         }
494         var result = convertToMemberType(null, map, type);
495         if (type.isBean() && result != null)
496            injectAnnotations((MarshalledMap) map, result);
497         return result;
498      }
499      return map;
500   }
501
502   private void injectAnnotations(MarshalledMap map, Object bean) throws ExecutableException {
503      var bm = toBeanMap(bean);
504      for (var entry : map.entrySet()) {
505         var key = entry.getKey();
506         var pm = bm.getPropertyMeta(key);
507         var val = pm == null ? null : getBeanValueSafely(bm, key);
508         if (pm == null || val == null)
509            continue;
510         var cm = (ClassMeta<?>) pm.getBeanInfo();
511         if (cm.getNameProperty() != null)
512            setName(cm, val, key);
513         if (cm.getParentProperty() != null)
514            setParent(cm, val, bean);
515         if (cm.isMap() && val instanceof Map<?, ?> val2 && !cm.getValueType().isObject() && cm.getValueType().getNameProperty() != null) {
516            var valueType = cm.getValueType();
517            for (Map.Entry<?, ?> e : ((Map<?, ?>) val2).entrySet())
518               setName(valueType, e.getValue(), e.getKey());
519         }
520      }
521   }
522
523   private static Object getBeanValueSafely(BeanMap<?> bm, String key) {
524      try {
525         return bm.get(key);
526      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Exception e) {
527         return null;
528      }
529   }
530}
